title: ROSMARINIC ACID
description: Rosmarinic acid is a natural polyphenolic compound found in plants of the Lamiaceae and Boraginaceae families, formed from caffeic acid and 3,4-dihydroxyphenyl lactic acid. It exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties by modulating inflammatory mediators, reducing oxidative stress, and influencing various cellular pathways including NF-κB and MAPK signaling. Rosmarinic acid has demonstrated therapeutic potential in inflammatory diseases, metabolic disorders, neuroprotection, and cancer prevention.

research.title: A Comprehensive Review of Rosmarinic Acid: From Phytochemistry to Pharmacology and Its New Insight
research.summary: Comprehensive review demonstrating rosmarinic acid's diverse biological activities including anti-inflammation, anti-oxidation, anti-diabetes, anti-virus, anti-tumor, neuroprotection, and hepatoprotection across multiple research fields.

research.title: A Review of the Anti-Inflammatory Effects of Rosmarinic Acid on Inflammatory Diseases
research.summary: Review examining rosmarinic acid's anti-inflammatory effects in various inflammatory diseases including arthritis, colitis, and atopic dermatitis through in vitro and in vivo studies, demonstrating its ability to modulate inflammatory mediators and cells.

research.title: Therapeutic Potential of Rosmarinic Acid: A Comprehensive Review
research.summary: Systematic review of rosmarinic acid's therapeutic applications showing efficacy in cancer, diabetes, inflammatory disorders, neurodegenerative diseases, and its anti-plasmodic, anti-viral, and anti-bacterial properties.

research.title: Biomedical Features and Therapeutic Potential of Rosmarinic Acid
research.summary: Analysis of rosmarinic acid's biomedical applications demonstrating multiple therapeutic benefits in cancer, diabetes, inflammatory disorders, neurodegenerative diseases, plus anti-oxidant capacity for potential use as a nutraceutical compound.

sentiment.summary: Research shows strong scientific evidence for rosmarinic acid's anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective effects across multiple conditions. However, challenges remain regarding its clinical applicability, bioavailability, and standardization of dosing protocols.

effects.label: Clinical Translation:
effects.description: Strong preclinical evidence with demonstrated effects in laboratory and animal studies, though human clinical data remains limited

effects.label: Multi-target Action:
effects.description: Works through multiple pathways including NF-κB inhibition, MAPK signaling modulation, and AMPK activation

effects.label: Disease Applications:
effects.description: Most effective for inflammatory conditions, metabolic disorders, and as adjunct therapy for various chronic diseases

effects.label: Bioavailability Concerns:
effects.description: Research indicates potential absorption and bioavailability challenges that may limit oral supplementation effectiveness

dosage.label: Research Doses:
dosage.description: Studies typically use 50-500mg doses, though optimal human dosing remains under investigation

dosage.label: Extract Concentration:
dosage.description: Effectiveness varies significantly based on source material and extraction method used

dosage.label: Timing Considerations:
dosage.description: May be most effective when taken with meals or vitamin C to enhance absorption

dosage.label: Individual Variation:
dosage.description: Response appears highly dependent on baseline inflammatory status and metabolic health

sideEffects.label: Generally Well-Tolerated:
sideEffects.description: Most studies report minimal adverse effects at standard supplemental doses

sideEffects.label: Toxicity Unknown:
sideEffects.description: Long-term safety data and toxicity thresholds in humans are not well-established

sideEffects.label: Drug Interactions:
sideEffects.description: Potential interactions with medications metabolized through similar pathways remain unexplored

sideEffects.label: Quality Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Contamination and standardization issues may affect supplement safety and efficacy

availability.label: Natural Sources:
availability.description: Found abundantly in rosemary, lemon balm, sage, oregano, and other herbs of Lamiaceae family

availability.label: Supplement Forms:
availability.description: Available as isolated extract or as component of herbal extracts with varying concentrations

availability.label: Regulatory Status:
availability.description: Generally recognized as safe when consumed as part of culinary herbs; supplement forms lack comprehensive regulation
